title: OpenStudyBuilder - Demonstration of Application
description:
Welcome to our YouTube video showcasing the incredible functionality of OpenStudyBuilder version 0.4! In this demonstration, we dive into the world of this open-source clinical metadata repository and study definition repository to unveil the immense potential of end-to-end automations.

The OpenStudyBuilder is an open-source project for clinical study evaluations. This tool is a new approach for working with studies that once fully implemented will drive end-to-end consistency and more efficient processes - all the way from protocol development and CRF design - to creation of datasets, analysis, reporting, submission to health authorities and public disclosure of study information.

Links:
- Project Homepage: https://openstudybuilder.com/
- Newsletter: https://www.linkedin.com/newsletters/openstudybuilder-6990328054849916928/
- GitLab Repository: https://gitlab.com/Novo-Nordisk/nn-public/openstudybuilder
author: Katja Glaß

title: DDF-1 OpenStudyBuilder as SDR Solution
description: The OpenStudyBuilder is an open-source MDR project. In scope of the TransCelerate DDF connectathon this video demonstrates the usage of the OpenStudyBuilder as a DDF SDR solution.
author: DDF Connectathon

title: DDF-2 OpenStudyBuilder for Standards and Protocol
description: The OpenStudyBuilder is an open-source MDR project. In scope of the TransCelerate DDF connectathon this video demonstrates how the standards are managed and how an integration for the Study Protocol could look like.
author: DDF Connectathon

title: DDF-3 OpenStudyBuilder with EDC Integrations
description: The OpenStudyBuilder is an open-source MDR project. In scope of the TransCelerate DDF connectathon this video demonstrates how the integration of the OpenStudyBuilder with EDC systems is working.
author: DDF Connectathon

title: CRF support in OpenStudyBuilder
description: This video demonstrates the Case Report Form (CRF) support within the OpenStudyBuilder. Learn how the OpenStudyBuilder implemented the CDISC Operational Data Model (ODM) to support the management of CRFs in the library part of this open source tool. See how to create templates, forms, itemGroups, items, how to link these items and finally how this can be displayed in an annoted or plain CRF.
author: Nicolas de Saint Jorre

title: Open Source Portal for Clinical Study Evaluations - a quick glance in 2021
description: How to find open source solutions like SAS macros, R scripts and other tools? The Open Source Portal for Clinical Study Evaluations is the answer! This video gives a quick overview on what the page is about and how you can find information there.
author: Katja Glaß

title: Novo Nordisk's Journey to an R based FDA Submission
description:
Abstract:
Join Novo Nordisk, leading pharmaceutical company in diabetes, obesity and other chronic diseases, as we celebrate reaching our NDA milestone, having recently submitted our first submission package featuring outputs exclusively written in R, along with select ADaM datasets also written in R. In this webinar, we will provide you with an in-depth look at our computing platform and our internal R package suite, including insights into their development. We will reflect on the process and setup we have in place and provide you with a sneak peek into what the future holds for us. You'll get an exclusive look at our journey and learn how we overcame challenges, as well as how we continue to innovate and streamline our processes. Don't miss this opportunity to gain insight into our approach to NDA submissions.

Speakers:

Ari Siggaard Knoph (MSc): 
Ari is a Principal Statistical Programmer and International Lead Programmer at Novo Nordisk with a seat on the pharmaverse council. He is a driving force behind the adoption of R in clinical deliverables and submissions at Novo Nordisk. Ari is also the author and maintainer of multiple R packages and leads the Novo Nordisk pharmaverse team.

Steffen Falgreen Larsen (MSc, PhD):
Steffen is a Statistical Specialist with over 8 years of experience in driving the shift towards R and Python at Novo Nordisk. He has a master's degree in mathematics with a specialization in life sciences and a PhD in biostatistics and informatics. Steffen has designed and co-developed a Statistical Computing Environment for R and the internally developed package ecosystem. He is a seasoned R user and developer.

Anders Ellern Bilgrau (MSc, PhD):
Anders is a Statistical Specialist at Novo Nordisk and an Adjunct Associate Professor at the Department of Mathematical Sciences, Aalborg University. He holds a master's degree in applied mathematics and a PhD in biostatistics and informatics. Anders is not only a skilled statistician but also an experienced software developer, with a special focus on R development for many years. He has played a key role in co-developing the R platform and internal packages used in Biostatistics at Novo Nordisk.
author: Ari Siggaard Knoph
